name: Jamaican Beef Patties
recipeIngredient: 1 Green bell pepper, stemmed, seeded and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 24 patties
Pastry:
Preheat the oven to 400°F. To make the dough:
- Place flour and salt in a large bowl; mix well.
- Cut shortening into small pieces about the size of walnuts. Add to the flour and, using your fingers, rub the flour and shortening together until it forms a coarse, mealy dough.
- Add ice water and gather the dough into a ball. The dough should be firm and not sticky. If too dry, add more water; if too sticky, add just enough flour to form a ball.
- Divide dough into 2 equal balls and cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or up to 2 days.
Filling:
In a large skillet, cook onion, garlic, and chilies in oil over moderate heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add beef, herbs, spices, bell pepper, and tomatoes; cook over high heat for 5 minutes, stirring constantly until thick and saucy. Add green onions and cook for 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper and cool to room temperature.
Assembly:
On a lightly floured surface, divide each dough ball into 2 equal balls (total of 4). Flatten into disc shapes, then divide each disc into 6 equal pieces and roll into balls. Roll each ball into a 3 1/2-inch diameter circle. Brush edges with beaten egg.
Place about 1 tablespoon filling on one side of each circle, leaving a 1/4-inch border. Fold dough over to form half-moon shapes. Seal edges with fork tines and brush remaining egg onto patties.
Bake:
Lightly grease a baking sheet. Arrange patties on the sheet and bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from oven and serve immediately.
